payload.description: Codex agent adapter: real-time PreToolUse gate + SessionStart context for OpenAI Codex CLI (portability axis B)

payload.reason: plan-review round 1 found 3 blockers: B1 codex shim tests targeted wrong file (claude regression is in integration suite); B2 gitignore change breaks 3 existing tests + needs sync --gitignore + committed .gitignore; B3 scope omitted root .gitignore and tests/integration/daemon/test_hook_entry.py. Plan doc fixed; re-submitting with corrected scope.

round-2 plan review converged: B1/B2/B3 blockers fixed in plan doc (codex shim integration test added, gitignore test-copy+sync-gitignore mandated, scope completed with .gitignore + integration test_hook_entry); merge generalization/shim/ABC/registry/backup logic all verified against real code interfaces.

checklist.note: Matcher is exactly `^(apply_patch|Edit|Write)$` (codex.py:30, asserted in test_codex.py:22). Codex shim denies via stdout JSON hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision="deny" and ALWAYS exits 0 (hook_entry.py:119-156); never exit 1/2. file=None state-gate confirmed: `_decide(tool, None)` at hook_entry.py:136, design §4.2/§8 verified file is cosmetic in the supervisor verdict. Hook command is absolute via shutil.which (codex.py:88-104). Trust-step lives in the AGENTS.md subsection (codex.py:42-46, "REQUIRED trust step ... run `/hooks`"). All 4 ABC abstractmethods (detect/install_hooks/inject_context/ agents_md_subsection) are implemented; on_uninstall/local_config_relpath/ installed_detail are non-abstract defaults (adapters/__init__.py:122-143).

`git diff main...HEAD` touches only the 19 files declared in the bundle's diff_in_scope (verified via --stat). No unrelated edits: every src change is adapter/shim/merge/registry/gitignore; every test file mirrors a changed src file; the two docs are the design+implementation plans. out_of_scope is empty and matches reality.

`_settings_merge` generalization preserves Claude behavior: matcher/marker default to `_MATCHER`/`_OURS_MARKER` and session marker to `_SESSION_OURS_MARKER` (_settings_merge.py:63-68,115-119); the two old named wrappers were folded into `_strip_entries(entries, marker)` with no dead code left. Existing Claude tests pass unchanged (full suite 1502 passed). install_hooks resolves BOTH binaries and raises RuntimeError BEFORE any write (codex.py:88-101; test_install_aborts asserts no file written). Idempotent/no-clobber/backup logic is reused intact from the audited Claude merge. Naming is consistent with the Claude adapter.

Malformed/non-object/missing-tool stdin → exit 0 fail-open ALLOW (hook_entry.py:128-135; test_codex_shim_malformed_stdin_fails_open). The `change resume` SessionStart marker is shared with Claude but benign: Codex writes `.codex/hooks.json` and Claude writes `.claude/settings.local.json` — separate files, no cross-strip (codex.py:103 vs claude_code.py). on_uninstall restores the EARLIEST backup (codex.py:144-152, _backup_sort_key; test_on_uninstall_restores_earliest_ backup green). Snapshot rollback in install_hooks bounds the whole write surface across BOTH merges via BaseException → _restore_snapshot (codex.py:107-116). Corrupt hooks.json fails loud: _read_settings raises ValueError, which is NOT caught by the BaseException handler's swallow (it re-raises after restore). Minor non-blocking note: a forced mid-merge failure can leave an orphan gitignored `*.super-harness-backup.*` file that snapshot-restore doesn't unlink — cosmetic only, not a correctness defect.

AGENTS.md is correctly NOT in the diff and does NOT contain the Codex subsection: this repo's adapters.yaml lists only superpowers+claude-code, and `_reinject_installed_adapters` (agents_md_render.py:170) renders subsections for INSTALLED adapters only — the Codex subsection ships as code in codex.py:35-67 and lands in a consuming repo's AGENTS.md on `adapter install codex`. `super-harness sync --check` reports in-sync and `doc check` is clean (no stale backtick code-references). Deferrals are recorded in private/OPEN-ITEMS.md (file-level scope from apply_patch body line 1021; narrower coverage vs WebSearch/non-shell line 1023). Design §2 summary phrase "matching apply_patch" is reconciled by §3.2/§3.3 which explicitly broaden the matcher to the edit-name union with rationale — not a contradiction.
